trucks.name: Silverado 1500
trucks.tagline: America's best-selling full-size truck. Available with a 2.7L Turbo-4, 5.3L V8, 6.2L V8, or available 3.0L Duramax diesel. Tows up to 13,300 lbs.

trucks.name: Silverado HD 2500/3500
trucks.tagline: Heavy-duty muscle built for serious work. Available 6.6L V8 gas or 6.6L Duramax diesel. Max towing up to 36,000 lbs on 3500 dually.

trucks.name: Colorado
trucks.tagline: Midsize truck, redesigned for 2023. 2.7L Turbo-4 across all trims. ZR2 brings serious off-road capability with Multimatic DSSV dampers.

suvs.name: Tahoe
suvs.tagline: Full-size SUV on a truck-based platform. 5.3L V8 standard, available 3.0L Duramax diesel. Three rows, 8,400-lb tow rating, available Super Cruise.

suvs.name: Suburban
suvs.tagline: The longest-running nameplate in automotive history. Shares Tahoe underpinnings with 22 additional inches of cargo space. The ultimate family hauler.

suvs.name: Traverse
suvs.tagline: Redesigned for 2024. Three-row crossover with a turbocharged 2.5L or available 2.0L turbo. Family-sized interior, up to 98.2 cu ft of cargo space.

suvs.name: Equinox
suvs.tagline: Chevy's best-selling crossover. 1.5L turbocharged 4-cylinder, available FWD or AWD. Practical, fuel-efficient, and packed with standard tech.

suvs.name: Blazer
suvs.tagline: Sport-forward midsize SUV with sharp styling. 2.0L turbocharged 4-cylinder or available 3.6L V6. Available AWD across all trims.

suvs.name: Trailblazer
suvs.tagline: Subcompact SUV with youthful styling and available AWD. 1.2L or 1.3L turbocharged 3-cylinder. Activ trim adds off-road-inspired looks.

suvs.name: Trax
suvs.tagline: Completely redesigned for 2024. The most affordable Chevy SUV with bold, angular styling. 1.2L turbocharged engine, FWD standard.

cars.name: Malibu
cars.tagline: Chevy's midsize sedan — a nameplate with deep history in the lineup. Turbocharged 1.5L engine, available hybrid powertrain.
cars.note: Production ends 2025. The Malibu nameplate dates to 1964.

sports.name: Corvette Stingray
sports.tagline: Mid-engine C8 generation. 6.2L LT2 V8 producing 490 hp (495 with performance exhaust). 0–60 in under 3 seconds. American supercar value.

sports.name: Corvette Z06
sports.tagline: 5.5L flat-plane crank LT6 V8 — 670 hp, 8,600 rpm redline. Race-derived naturally aspirated engine. Available Z07 track package. Wider body, carbon fiber bodywork.

sports.name: Corvette E-Ray
sports.tagline: First-ever all-wheel drive Corvette. Combines 6.2L LT2 V8 with a front electric motor — 655 hp combined. 0–60 in 2.5 seconds. First Corvette hybrid production model.

sports.name: Camaro
sports.tagline: Six generations of American muscle. Available 2.0T, 3.6L V6, 6.2L V8 (SS/ZL1). The ZL1 supercharged 650 hp was the final high point.
sports.note: Production ended Feb 2024. Future revival expected — stay tuned.

evs.name: Equinox EV
evs.tagline: Built on GM's Ultium platform. Up to 319 miles of estimated range. Available FWD or AWD. One of the most affordable long-range EVs on the market.

evs.name: Blazer EV
evs.tagline: Performance-focused EV on the Ultium platform. SS trim delivers 557 hp AWD. Up to 320 miles estimated range. DC fast charging standard.

evs.name: Silverado EV
evs.tagline: Purpose-built electric truck on the Ultium platform. RST edition: 754 hp, 400+ miles estimated range. Multi-Flex Midgate, 10.2 kW bidirectional charging capability.

classics.name: Chevelle SS 454
classics.cultural: The pinnacle of the muscle car era. The LS6 454 variant — 450 hp, solid-lifter cam, 11.25:1 compression — is one of the most sought-after factory muscle cars ever built.

classics.name: '69 Camaro Z/28 · SS · ZL1
classics.cultural: The most iconic first-gen Camaro. The COPO ZL1 — 69 units built with an all-aluminum 427 — remains one of the rarest and most valuable American muscle cars ever made.

classics.name: '64 Impala SS
classics.cultural: The cornerstone of lowrider culture, immortalized in West Coast hip-hop and Chicano art. Its long hood and smooth lines made it the canvas for generations of custom builders.

classics.name: '57 Bel Air
classics.cultural: Perhaps the most recognizable American car ever built. The fuel-injected 283 was a milestone — the first production engine to achieve 1 horsepower per cubic inch.

classics.name: C10 — Square Body
classics.cultural: The square-body C10 is the most universally beloved classic truck in America. Clean lines, massive aftermarket support, and cultural versatility across lowrider, farm truck, and restomod builds.

classics.name: K5 Blazer — First Gen
classics.cultural: The K5 pioneered the full-size SUV before the term existed — removable hardtop, real 4WD, and truck-based capability. The spiritual ancestor of every Bronco and 4Runner that followed.

classics.name: Nova SS
classics.cultural: Big-block muscle in a lighter, smaller package. The 396-equipped Nova was a legitimate quarter-mile threat and remains one of the most accessible entry points into classic Chevy muscle.

classics.name: El Camino SS
classics.cultural: Half car, half truck — entirely American. Built on the A-body Chevelle platform, the El Camino SS could be optioned with the same big-block engines as its muscle car siblings.

classics.name: C3 Corvette — Shark Era
classics.cultural: The Shark-era Corvette. LT1, L88, ZR1 — factory race engines in a road car. The 1969–72 big-block cars are the performance peak of the generation. The L88 was never meant to be street driven.

classics.name: Monte Carlo SS
classics.cultural: Chevy's personal luxury coupe with muscle car underpinnings. The SS 454 first-gen is a sleeper collectible. The 1980s aero-body NASCAR SS version is a certified piece of racing heritage.
